31_1_1.NKJV: The vision of Obadiah. Thus says the Lord GOD concerning Edom (We have heard a report from the LORD, And a messenger has been sent among the nations, saying, “Arise, and let us rise up against her for battle”):
31_1_1.KJV: The vision<S>2377</S> of Obadiah<S>5662</S>. Thus saith<S>559</S> the Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S> concerning Edom<S>123</S>; We have heard<S>8085</S> a rumour<S>8052</S> from the LORD<S>3068</S>, and an ambassador<S>6735</S> is sent<S>7971</S> among the heathen<S>1471</S>, Arise<S>6965</S> ye, and let us rise up<S>6965</S> against her in battle<S>4421</S>.
31_1_1.ESV: The vision of Obadiah. Edom Will Be Humbled Thus says the Lord GOD concerning Edom: We have heard a report from the LORD, and a messenger has been sent among the nations: “Rise up! Let us rise against her for battle!”
31_1_1.NASB: The vision of Obadiah. Thus says the Lord GOD concerning Edom-- We have heard a report from the LORD, And an envoy has been sent among the nations [saying], 'Arise and let us go against her for battle '--
31_1_1.NLT:
This is the vision that the Sovereign LORD revealed to Obadiah concerning the land of Edom. Edom’s Judgment Announced We have heard a message from the LORD
that an ambassador was sent to the nations to say,
“Get ready, everyone!
Let’s assemble our armies and attack Edom!”
31_1_1.WEB: The vision of Obadiah. This is what the Lord  Yahweh  says about Edom. We have heard news from Yahweh, and an ambassador is sent among the nations, saying, “Arise, and let’s rise up against her in battle.
31_1_1.CSB17:
The vision of Obadiah. This is what the Lord GOD has said about Edom: ⓒ We have heard a message from the LORD; ⓓ
an envoy has been sent ⓔ among the nations:
“Rise up, ⓕ and let us go to war against her.” [1]
31_1_1.AMP:
The vision of Obadiah.
Thus says the Lord GOD concerning Edom—
We have heard a report from the LORD,
And an ambassador has been sent among the nations, saying,
" Arise, and let us rise up against Edom for battle [with the LORD as commander]!"
31_1_1.MSG:
Obadiah's Message to Edom
from God, the Master.
We got the news straight from God
by a special messenger sent out to the godless nations:
"On your feet, prepare for battle;
get ready to make war on Edom!
31_1_1.NIV:
The vision of Obadiah.
This is what the Sovereign Lord says about Edom—
We have heard a message from the Lord:
An envoy was sent to the nations to say,
“Rise, and let us go against her for battle”—
31_1_1.NRSVCE:
The vision of Obadiah. Thus says the Lord GOD concerning Edom:
We have heard a report from the LORD,
and a messenger has been sent among the nations:
“Rise up! Let us rise against it for battle!”

31_1_2.NKJV: “Behold, I will make you small among the nations; You shall be greatly despised.
31_1_2.KJV: Behold, I have made<S>5414</S> thee small<S>6996</S> among the heathen<S>1471</S>: thou art greatly<S>3966</S> despised<S>959</S>.
31_1_2.ESV: Behold, I will make you small among the nations; you shall be utterly despised.
31_1_2.NASB: 'Behold, I will make you small among the nations; You are greatly despised.
31_1_2.NLT:
The LORD says to Edom,
“I will cut you down to size among the nations;
you will be greatly despised.
31_1_2.WEB: Behold,  I have made you small among the nations. You are greatly despised.
31_1_2.CSB17:
Look, I will make you insignificant ⓖ
among the nations;
you will be deeply despised.
31_1_2.AMP:
"Behold [Edom], I shall [humiliate you and] make you small among the nations;
You are greatly despised.
31_1_2.MSG:
"Listen to this, Edom:
I'm turning you to a no-account,
the runt of the godless nations, despised.
31_1_2.NIV:
“See, I will make you small among the nations;
you will be utterly despised.
31_1_2.NRSVCE:
I will surely make you least among the nations;
you shall be utterly despised.

31_1_3.NKJV: The pride of your heart has deceived you, You who dwell in the clefts of the rock, Whose habitation is high; You who say in your heart, ‘Who will bring me down to the ground?’
31_1_3.KJV: The pride<S>2087</S> of thine heart<S>3820</S> hath deceived<S>5377</S> thee, thou that dwellest<S>7931</S> in the clefts<S>2288</S> of the rock<S>5553</S>, whose habitation<S>3427</S> is high<S>4791</S>; that saith<S>559</S> in his heart<S>3820</S>, Who shall bring me down<S>3381</S> to the ground<S>776</S>?
31_1_3.ESV: The pride of your heart has deceived you, you who live in the clefts of the rock,  in your lofty dwelling, who say in your heart, “Who will bring me down to the ground?”
31_1_3.NASB: 'The arrogance of your heart has deceived you, You who live in the clefts of the rock, In the loftiness of your dwelling place, Who say in your heart, 'Who will bring me down to earth?'
31_1_3.NLT:
You have been deceived by your own pride
because you live in a rock fortress
and make your home high in the mountains.
‘Who can ever reach us way up here?’
you ask boastfully.
31_1_3.WEB: The pride of your heart has deceived you, you who dwell in the clefts of the rock, whose habitation is high, who says in his heart, ‘Who will bring me down to the ground?’
31_1_3.CSB17:
Your arrogant heart has deceived you, ⓗ
you who live in clefts of the rock [2] [3] ⓘ
in your home on the heights,
who say to yourself,
“Who can bring me down to the ground?” ⓙ
31_1_3.AMP:
"The pride and arrogance of your heart have deceived you,
You who live in the clefts and lofty security of the rock (Sela),
Whose dwelling place is high,
Who say [boastfully] in your heart,
'Who will bring me down to earth?'
31_1_3.MSG:
You thought you were so great,
perched high among the rocks, king of the mountain,
Thinking to yourself,
'Nobody can get to me! Nobody can touch me!'
31_1_3.NIV:
The pride of your heart has deceived you,
you who live in the clefts of the rocks
and make your home on the heights,
you who say to yourself,
‘Who can bring me down to the ground?’
31_1_3.NRSVCE:
Your proud heart has deceived you,
you that live in the clefts of the rock,
whose dwelling is in the heights.
You say in your heart,
“Who will bring me down to the ground?”

31_1_4.NKJV: Though you ascend as high as the eagle, And though you set your nest among the stars, From there I will bring you down,” says the LORD.
31_1_4.KJV: Though thou exalt<S>1361</S> thyself as the eagle<S>5404</S>, and though thou set<S>7760</S> thy nest<S>7064</S> among the stars<S>3556</S>, thence will I bring thee down<S>3381</S>, saith<S>5002</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>.
31_1_4.ESV: Though you soar aloft like the eagle, though your nest is set among the stars, from there I will bring you down, declares the LORD.
31_1_4.NASB: 'Though you build high like the eagle, Though you set your nest among the stars, From there I will bring you down,' declares the LORD.
31_1_4.NLT:
But even if you soar as high as eagles
and build your nest among the stars,
I will bring you crashing down,”
says the LORD.
31_1_4.WEB: Though you mount on high as the eagle, and though your nest is set among the stars, I will bring you down from there,” says Yahweh.
31_1_4.CSB17:
Though you seem to soar [4] like an eagle ⓚ
and make your nest among the stars, ⓛ
even from there I will bring you down. This is the LORD’s declaration.
31_1_4.AMP:
"Though you build [your nest] on high like the eagle,
Though you set your nest among the stars,
I will bring you down from there," says the LORD.
31_1_4.MSG:
Think again. Even if, like an eagle,
you hang out on a high cliff-face,
Even if you build your nest in the stars,
I'll bring you down to earth."
God's sure Word.
31_1_4.NIV:
Though you soar like the eagle
and make your nest among the stars,
from there I will bring you down,”
declares the Lord.
31_1_4.NRSVCE:
Though you soar aloft like the eagle,
though your nest is set among the stars,
from there I will bring you down,
— says the LORD.

31_1_5.NKJV: “If thieves had come to you, If robbers by night— Oh, how you will be cut off!— Would they not have stolen till they had enough? If grape-gatherers had come to you, Would they not have left some gleanings?
31_1_5.KJV: If thieves<S>1590</S> came<S>935</S> to thee, if robbers<S>7703</S> by night<S>3915</S>, (how art thou cut off<S>1820</S>!) would they not have stolen<S>1589</S> till they had enough<S>1767</S>? if the grapegatherers<S>1219</S> came<S>935</S> to thee, would they not leave<S>7604</S> some grapes<S>5955</S>? <sup>some: or, gleanings?</sup>
31_1_5.ESV: If thieves came to you, if plunderers came by night — how you have been destroyed! — would they not steal only enough for themselves? If grape gatherers came to you, would they not leave gleanings?
31_1_5.NASB: 'If thieves came to you, If robbers by night-- O how you will be ruined!-- Would they not steal [only] until they had enough? If grape gatherers came to you, Would they not leave [some] gleanings?
31_1_5.NLT:
“If thieves came at night and robbed you
(what a disaster awaits you!),
they would not take everything.
Those who harvest grapes
always leave a few for the poor.
But your enemies will wipe you out completely!
31_1_5.WEB: “If thieves came to you, if robbers by night — oh, what disaster awaits you — wouldn’t they only steal until they had enough? If grape pickers came to you, wouldn’t they leave some gleaning grapes?
31_1_5.CSB17:
If thieves came to you, ⓜ
if marauders by night —
how ravaged you would be! —
wouldn’t they steal only what they wanted?
If grape pickers came to you,
wouldn’t they leave some grapes? ⓝ
31_1_5.AMP:
"If thieves came to you,
If robbers by night—
How you will be ruined!—
Would they not steal only until they had enough?
If grape gatherers came to you,
Would they not leave some grapes for gleaning?
31_1_5.MSG:
"If thieves crept up on you,
they'd rob you blind—isn't that so?
If they mugged you on the streets at night,
they'd pick you clean—isn't that so?
31_1_5.NIV:
“If thieves came to you,
if robbers in the night—
Oh, what a disaster awaits you—
would they not steal only as much as they wanted?
If grape pickers came to you,
would they not leave a few grapes?
31_1_5.NRSVCE:
If thieves came to you,
if plunderers by night
— how you have been destroyed! —
would they not steal only what they wanted?
If grape-gatherers came to you,
would they not leave gleanings?

31_1_6.NKJV: “Oh, how Esau shall be searched out! How his hidden treasures shall be sought after!
31_1_6.KJV: How are the things of Esau<S>6215</S> searched out<S>2664</S>! how are his hidden<S>4710</S> things sought up<S>1158</S>!
31_1_6.ESV: How Esau has been pillaged, his treasures sought out!
31_1_6.NASB: 'O how Esau will be ransacked, [And] his hidden treasures searched out!
31_1_6.NLT:
Every nook and cranny of Edom
will be searched and looted.
Every treasure will be found and taken.
31_1_6.WEB: How Esau will be ransacked! How his hidden treasures are sought out!
31_1_6.CSB17:
How Esau will be pillaged, ⓐ
his hidden treasures searched out! ⓑ
31_1_6.AMP:
"How Esau (Edom) shall be ransacked [by men who come to ravage with divine approval]!
How his hidden treasures shall be searched out!
31_1_6.MSG:
Oh, they'll take Esau apart, piece by piece,
empty his purse and pockets.
31_1_6.NIV:
But how Esau will be ransacked,
his hidden treasures pillaged!
31_1_6.NRSVCE:
How Esau has been pillaged,
his treasures searched out!

31_1_7.NKJV: All the men in your confederacy Shall force you to the border; The men at peace with you Shall deceive you and prevail against you. Those who eat your bread shall lay a trap for you. No one is aware of it.
31_1_7.KJV: All the men<S>582</S> of thy confederacy<S>1285</S> have brought<S>7971</S> thee even to the border<S>1366</S>: the men<S>582</S> that were at peace<S>7965</S> with thee have deceived<S>5377</S> thee, and prevailed<S>3201</S> against thee; they that eat thy bread<S>3899</S> have laid<S>7760</S> a wound<S>4204</S> under thee: there is none understanding<S>8394</S> in him. <sup>that were: Heb. of thy peace</sup> <sup>they: Heb. the men of thy bread</sup> <sup>in him: or, of it</sup>
31_1_7.ESV: All your allies have driven you to your border; those at peace with you have deceived you; they have prevailed against you; those who eat your bread  have set a trap beneath you — you have  no understanding.
31_1_7.NASB: 'All the men allied with you Will send you forth to the border, And the men at peace with you Will deceive you and overpower you. [They who eat] your bread Will set an ambush for you. (There is no understanding in him.)
31_1_7.NLT:
“All your allies will turn against you.
They will help to chase you from your land.
They will promise you peace
while plotting to deceive and destroy you.
Your trusted friends will set traps for you,
and you won’t even know about it.
31_1_7.WEB: All the men of your alliance have brought you on your way, even to the border. The men who were at peace with you have deceived you, and prevailed against you. Friends who eat your bread lay a snare under you. There is no understanding in him.”
31_1_7.CSB17:
Everyone who has a treaty with you ⓒ
will drive you to the border;
everyone at peace with you
will deceive and conquer you.
Those who eat your bread ⓓ
will set [5] a trap for you.
He will be unaware of it. ⓔ
31_1_7.AMP:
"All the men allied with you
Shall send you on your way to the border;
The men who were at peace with you
Shall deceive you and overpower you;
Those who eat your bread [those you trust]
Shall set a hostile ambush for you.
(There is no understanding of it.)
31_1_7.MSG:
All your old partners will drive you to the edge.
Your old friends will lie to your face.
Your old drinking buddies will stab you in the back.
Your world will collapse. You won't know what hit you.
31_1_7.NIV:
All your allies will force you to the border;
your friends will deceive and overpower you;
those who eat your bread will set a trap for you,
but you will not detect it.
31_1_7.NRSVCE:
All your allies have deceived you,
they have driven you to the border;
your confederates have prevailed against you;
those who ate your bread have set a trap for you —
there is no understanding of it.

31_1_8.NKJV: “Will I not in that day,” says the LORD, “Even destroy the wise men from Edom, And understanding from the mountains of Esau?
31_1_8.KJV: Shall I not in that day<S>3117</S>, saith<S>5002</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>, even destroy<S>6</S> the wise<S>2450</S> men out of Edom<S>123</S>, and understanding<S>8394</S> out of the mount<S>2022</S> of Esau<S>6215</S>?
31_1_8.ESV: Will I not on that day, declares the LORD, destroy the wise men out of Edom, and understanding out of Mount Esau?
31_1_8.NASB: 'Will I not on that day,' declares the LORD, 'Destroy wise men from Edom And understanding from the mountain of Esau?
31_1_8.NLT:
At that time not a single wise person
will be left in the whole land of Edom,”
says the LORD.
“For on the mountains of Edom
I will destroy everyone who has understanding.
31_1_8.WEB: “Won’t I in that day”, says Yahweh, “destroy the wise men out of Edom, and understanding out of the mountain of Esau?
31_1_8.CSB17:
In that day — this is the LORD’s declaration — will I not eliminate the wise ones of Edom ⓕ
and those who understand
from the hill country of Esau?
31_1_8.AMP:
"Will I not on that day," says the LORD,
"Destroy the wise men from Edom [removing all wisdom]
And understanding from the mountain of Esau?
31_1_8.MSG:
So don't be surprised"—it's God's sure Word!—
"when I wipe out all sages from Edom
and rid the Esau mountains of its famous wise men.
31_1_8.NIV:
“In that day,” declares the Lord,
“will I not destroy the wise men of Edom,
men of understanding in the mountains of Esau?
31_1_8.NRSVCE:
On that day, says the LORD,
I will destroy the wise out of Edom,
and understanding out of Mount Esau.

31_1_9.NKJV: Then your mighty men, O Teman, shall be dismayed, To the end that everyone from the mountains of Esau May be cut off by slaughter.
31_1_9.KJV: And thy mighty<S>1368</S> men, O Teman<S>8487</S>, shall be dismayed<S>2865</S>, to the end that every one<S>376</S> of the mount<S>2022</S> of Esau<S>6215</S> may be cut off<S>3772</S> by slaughter<S>6993</S>.
31_1_9.ESV: And your mighty men shall be dismayed, O Teman, so that every man from Mount Esau will be cut off by slaughter.
31_1_9.NASB: 'Then your mighty men will be dismayed, O Teman, So that everyone may be cut off from the mountain of Esau by slaughter.
31_1_9.NLT:
The mightiest warriors of Teman
will be terrified,
and everyone on the mountains of Edom
will be cut down in the slaughter.
31_1_9.WEB: Your mighty men, Teman, will be dismayed, to the end that everyone may be cut off from the mountain of Esau by slaughter.
31_1_9.CSB17:
Teman, [6] ⓖ your warriors ⓗ will be terrified
so that everyone from the hill country of Esau
will be destroyed by slaughter. ⓘ
31_1_9.AMP:
"And your mighty men shall be dismayed and demoralized, O Teman,
So that everyone from the mountain of Esau may be cut off in the slaughter.
31_1_9.MSG:
Your great heroes will desert you, Teman.
There'll be nobody left in Esau's mountains.
31_1_9.NIV:
Your warriors, O Teman, will be terrified,
and everyone in Esau’s mountains
will be cut down in the slaughter.
31_1_9.NRSVCE:
Your warriors shall be shattered, O Teman,
so that everyone from Mount Esau will be cut off.

31_1_10.NKJV: “For violence against your brother Jacob, Shame shall cover you, And you shall be cut off forever.
31_1_10.KJV: For thy violence<S>2555</S> against thy brother<S>251</S> Jacob<S>3290</S> shame<S>955</S> shall cover<S>3680</S> thee, and thou shalt be cut off<S>3772</S> for ever<S>5769</S>.
31_1_10.ESV: Because of the violence done to your brother Jacob, shame shall cover you, and you shall be cut off forever.
31_1_10.NASB: 'Because of violence to your brother Jacob, You will be covered [with] shame, And you will be cut off forever.
31_1_10.NLT:
“Because of the violence you did
to your close relatives in Israel,
you will be filled with shame
and destroyed forever.
31_1_10.WEB: For the violence done to your brother Jacob, shame will cover you, and you will be cut off forever.
31_1_10.CSB17:
You will be covered with shame
and destroyed forever
because of violence done to your brother Jacob. ⓙ
31_1_10.AMP:
"Because of the violence you did against your brother Jacob,
Shame shall cover you [completely],
And you shall be cut off forever.
31_1_10.MSG:
Because of the murderous history compiled
against your brother Jacob,
You will be looked down on by everyone.
You'll lose your place in history.
31_1_10.NIV:
Because of the violence against your brother Jacob,
you will be covered with shame;
you will be destroyed forever.
31_1_10.NRSVCE:
For the slaughter and violence done to your brother Jacob,
shame shall cover you,
and you shall be cut off forever.

31_1_11.NKJV: In the day that you stood on the other side— In the day that strangers carried captive his forces, When foreigners entered his gates And cast lots for Jerusalem— Even you were as one of them.
31_1_11.KJV: In the day<S>3117</S> that thou stoodest<S>5975</S> on the other side, in the day<S>3117</S> that the strangers<S>2114</S> carried away captive<S>7617</S> his forces<S>2428</S>, and foreigners<S>5237</S> entered<S>935</S> into his gates<S>8179</S>, and cast<S>3032</S> lots<S>1486</S> upon Jerusalem<S>3389</S>, even thou wast as one<S>259</S> of them. <sup>captive: or, his substance</sup>
31_1_11.ESV: On the day that you stood aloof, on the day that strangers carried off his wealth and foreigners entered his gates and cast lots for Jerusalem, you were like one of them.
31_1_11.NASB: 'On the day that you stood aloof, On the day that strangers carried off his wealth, And foreigners entered his gate And cast lots for Jerusalem-- You too were as one of them.
31_1_11.NLT:
When they were invaded,
you stood aloof, refusing to help them.
Foreign invaders carried off their wealth
and cast lots to divide up Jerusalem,
but you acted like one of Israel’s enemies.
31_1_11.WEB: In the day that you stood on the other side, in the day that strangers carried away his substance, and foreigners entered into his gates, and cast lots for Jerusalem, even you were like one of them.
31_1_11.CSB17:
On the day you stood aloof, ⓚ
on the day strangers captured his wealth, [7]
while foreigners entered his city gate
and cast lots for Jerusalem, ⓛ
you were just like one of them. ⓜ
31_1_11.AMP:
"On the day that [Jerusalem was destroyed] you stood aloof [from your brother Jacob]—
On the day that strangers took his forces captive and carried off his wealth,
And foreigners entered his gates
And cast lots for Jerusalem [dividing the city for plunder]—
You too were like one of them [collaborating with the enemy].
31_1_11.MSG:
On that day you stood there and didn't do anything.
Strangers took your brother's army into exile.
Godless foreigners invaded and pillaged Jerusalem.
You stood there and watched.
You were as bad as they were.
31_1_11.NIV:
On the day you stood aloof
while strangers carried off his wealth
and foreigners entered his gates
and cast lots for Jerusalem,
you were like one of them.
31_1_11.NRSVCE:
On the day that you stood aside,
on the day that strangers carried off his wealth,
and foreigners entered his gates
and cast lots for Jerusalem,
you too were like one of them.

31_1_12.NKJV: “But you should not have gazed on the day of your brother In the day of his captivity; Nor should you have rejoiced over the children of Judah In the day of their destruction; Nor should you have spoken proudly In the day of distress.
31_1_12.KJV: But thou shouldest not have looked<S>7200</S> on the day<S>3117</S> of thy brother<S>251</S> in the day<S>3117</S> that he became a stranger<S>5235</S>; neither shouldest thou have rejoiced<S>8055</S> over the children<S>1121</S> of Judah<S>3063</S> in the day<S>3117</S> of their destruction<S>6</S>; neither shouldest thou have spoken<S>6310</S> proudly<S>1431</S> in the day<S>3117</S> of distress<S>6869</S>. <sup>spoken: Heb. magnified thy mouth</sup>
31_1_12.ESV: But do not gloat over the day of your brother in the day of his misfortune; do not rejoice over the people of Judah in the day of their ruin; do not boast  in the day of distress.
31_1_12.NASB: 'Do not gloat over your brother's day, The day of his misfortune. And do not rejoice over the sons of Judah In the day of their destruction; Yes, do not boast In the day of [their] distress.
31_1_12.NLT:
“You should not have gloated
when they exiled your relatives to distant lands.
You should not have rejoiced
when the people of Judah suffered such misfortune.
You should not have spoken arrogantly
in that terrible time of trouble.
31_1_12.WEB: But don’t look down on your brother in the day of his disaster, and don’t rejoice over the children of Judah in the day of their destruction. Don’t speak proudly in the day of distress.
31_1_12.CSB17:
Do not [8] gloat over your brother ⓝ
in the day of his calamity;
do not rejoice over the people of Judah ⓐ
in the day of their destruction;
do not boastfully mock [9] ⓑ
in the day of distress. ⓒ
31_1_12.AMP:
"Do not gaze and gloat [in triumph] over your brother's day,
The day when his misfortune came.
Do not rejoice over the sons of Judah
In the day of their destruction;
Do not speak arrogantly [jeering and maliciously mocking]
In the day of their distress.
31_1_12.MSG:
You shouldn't have gloated over your brother
when he was down-and-out.
You shouldn't have laughed and joked at Judah's sons
when they were facedown in the mud.
You shouldn't have talked so big
when everything was so bad.
31_1_12.NIV:
You should not look down on your brother
in the day of his misfortune,
nor rejoice over the people of Judah
in the day of their destruction,
nor boast so much
in the day of their trouble.
31_1_12.NRSVCE:
But you should not have gloated over your brother
on the day of his misfortune;
you should not have rejoiced over the people of Judah
on the day of their ruin;
you should not have boasted
on the day of distress.

31_1_13.NKJV: You should not have entered the gate of My people In the day of their calamity. Indeed, you should not have gazed on their affliction In the day of their calamity, Nor laid hands on their substance In the day of their calamity.
31_1_13.KJV: Thou shouldest not have entered<S>935</S> into the gate<S>8179</S> of my people<S>5971</S> in the day<S>3117</S> of their calamity<S>343</S>; yea, thou shouldest not have looked<S>7200</S> on their affliction<S>7451</S> in the day<S>3117</S> of their calamity<S>343</S>, nor have laid<S>7971</S> hands on their substance<S>2428</S> in the day<S>3117</S> of their calamity<S>343</S>; <sup>substance: or, forces</sup>
31_1_13.ESV: Do not enter the gate of my people in the day of their calamity; do not gloat over his disaster in the day of his calamity; do not loot his wealth in the day of his calamity.
31_1_13.NASB: 'Do not enter the gate of My people In the day of their disaster. Yes, you, do not gloat over their calamity In the day of their disaster. And do not loot their wealth In the day of their disaster.
31_1_13.NLT:
You should not have plundered the land of Israel
when they were suffering such calamity.
You should not have gloated over their destruction
when they were suffering such calamity.
You should not have seized their wealth
when they were suffering such calamity.
31_1_13.WEB: Don’t enter into the gate of my people in the day of their calamity. Don’t look down on their affliction in the day of their calamity, neither seize their wealth on the day of their calamity.
31_1_13.CSB17:
Do not enter my people’s city gate
in the day of their disaster. ⓓ
Yes, you — do not gloat over their misery
in the day of their disaster,
and do not appropriate their possessions ⓔ
in the day of their disaster.
31_1_13.AMP:
"Do not enter the gate of My people
In the day of their disaster;
Yes, you, do not look [with delight] on their misery
In the day of their ruin,
And do not loot treasures
In the day of their ruin.
31_1_13.MSG:
You shouldn't have taken advantage of my people
when their lives had fallen apart.
You of all people should not have been amused
by their troubles, their wrecked nation.
You shouldn't have taken the shirt off their back
when they were knocked flat, defenseless.
31_1_13.NIV:
You should not march through the gates of my people
in the day of their disaster,
nor look down on them in their calamity
in the day of their disaster,
nor seize their wealth
in the day of their disaster.
31_1_13.NRSVCE:
You should not have entered the gate of my people
on the day of their calamity;
you should not have joined in the gloating over Judah’s disaster
on the day of his calamity;
you should not have looted his goods
on the day of his calamity.

31_1_14.NKJV: You should not have stood at the crossroads To cut off those among them who escaped; Nor should you have delivered up those among them who remained In the day of distress.
31_1_14.KJV: Neither shouldest thou have stood<S>5975</S> in the crossway<S>6563</S>, to cut off<S>3772</S> those of his that did escape<S>6412</S>; neither shouldest thou have delivered up<S>5462</S> those of his that did remain<S>8300</S> in the day<S>3117</S> of distress<S>6869</S>. <sup>delivered up: or, shut up</sup>
31_1_14.ESV: Do not stand at the crossroads to cut off his fugitives; do not hand over his survivors in the day of distress.
31_1_14.NASB: 'Do not stand at the fork of the road To cut down their fugitives; And do not imprison their survivors In the day of their distress.
31_1_14.NLT:
You should not have stood at the crossroads,
killing those who tried to escape.
You should not have captured the survivors
and handed them over in their terrible time of trouble.
31_1_14.WEB: Don’t stand in the crossroads to cut off those of his who escape. Don’t deliver up those of his who remain in the day of distress.
31_1_14.CSB17:
Do not stand at the crossroads [10] ⓕ
to cut off their fugitives,
and do not hand over their survivors
in the day of distress.
31_1_14.AMP:
"Do not stand at the crossroad
To cut down those [of Judah] who escaped;
And do not hand over [to the enemy] those [of Judah] who survive
In the day of their distress.
31_1_14.MSG:
And you shouldn't have stood waiting at the outskirts
and cut off refugees,
And traitorously turned in helpless survivors
who had lost everything.
31_1_14.NIV:
You should not wait at the crossroads
to cut down their fugitives,
nor hand over their survivors
in the day of their trouble.
31_1_14.NRSVCE:
You should not have stood at the crossings
to cut off his fugitives;
you should not have handed over his survivors
on the day of distress.

31_1_15.NKJV: “For the day of the LORD upon all the nations is near; As you have done, it shall be done to you; Your reprisal shall return upon your own head.
31_1_15.KJV: For the day<S>3117</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> is near<S>7138</S> upon all the heathen<S>1471</S>: as thou hast done<S>6213</S>, it shall be done<S>6213</S> unto thee: thy reward<S>1576</S> shall return<S>7725</S> upon thine own head<S>7218</S>.
31_1_15.ESV: For the day of the LORD is near upon all the nations. As you have done, it shall be done to you; your deeds shall return on your own head.
31_1_15.NASB: 'For the day of the LORD draws near on all the nations. As you have done, it will be done to you. Your dealings will return on your own head.
31_1_15.NLT:
“The day is near when I, the LORD,
will judge all godless nations!
As you have done to Israel,
so it will be done to you.
All your evil deeds
will fall back on your own heads.
31_1_15.WEB: For the day of Yahweh is near all the nations! As you have done, it will be done to you. Your deeds will return upon your own head.
31_1_15.CSB17:
For the day of the LORD ⓖ is near,
against all the nations. ⓗ
As you have done, ⓘ it will be done to you;
what you deserve will return on your own head. ⓙ
31_1_15.AMP:
"For the [judgment] day of the LORD draws near on all the nations.
As you have done, it shall be done to you [in retribution];
Your [evil] dealings will return on your own head.
31_1_15.MSG:
"God's Judgment Day is near
for all the godless nations.
As you have done, it will be done to you.
What you did will boomerang back
and hit your own head.
31_1_15.NIV:
“The day of the Lord is near
for all nations.
As you have done, it will be done to you;
your deeds will return upon your own head.
31_1_15.NRSVCE:
For the day of the LORD is near against all the nations.
As you have done, it shall be done to you;
your deeds shall return on your own head.

31_1_16.NKJV: For as you drank on My holy mountain, So shall all the nations drink continually; Yes, they shall drink, and swallow, And they shall be as though they had never been.
31_1_16.KJV: For as ye have drunk<S>8354</S> upon my holy<S>6944</S> mountain<S>2022</S>, so shall all the heathen<S>1471</S> drink<S>8354</S> continually<S>8548</S>, yea, they shall drink<S>8354</S>, and they shall swallow down<S>3886</S>, and they shall be as though they had not<S>3808</S> been. <sup>swallow: or, sup up</sup>
31_1_16.ESV: For as you have drunk on my holy mountain, so all the nations shall drink continually; they shall drink and swallow, and shall be as though they had never been.
31_1_16.NASB: 'Because just as you drank on My holy mountain, All the nations will drink continually. They will drink and swallow And become as if they had never existed.
31_1_16.NLT:
Just as you swallowed up my people
on my holy mountain,
so you and the surrounding nations
will swallow the punishment I pour out on you.
Yes, all you nations will drink and stagger
and disappear from history.
31_1_16.WEB: For as you have drunk on my holy mountain, so will all the nations drink continually. Yes, they will drink, swallow down, and will be as though they had not been.
31_1_16.CSB17:
As you have drunk on my holy mountain, ⓚ
so all the nations will drink continually. ⓛ
They will drink and gulp down
and be as though they had never been.
31_1_16.AMP:
"Because just as you [Edom] drank on My holy mountain [desecrating it in the revelry of the destroyers],
So shall all the nations drink continually [one by one, of My wrath];
Yes, they shall drink and swallow [the full measure of punishment]
And become as though they had never existed.
31_1_16.MSG:
Just as you partied on my holy mountain,
all the godless nations will drink God's wrath.
They'll drink and drink and drink—
they'll drink themselves to death.
31_1_16.NIV:
Just as you drank on my holy hill,
so all the nations will drink continually;
they will drink and drink
and be as if they had never been.
31_1_16.NRSVCE:
For as you have drunk on my holy mountain,
all the nations around you shall drink;
they shall drink and gulp down,
and shall be as though they had never been.

31_1_17.NKJV: “But on Mount Zion there shall be deliverance, And there shall be holiness; The house of Jacob shall possess their possessions.
31_1_17.KJV: But upon mount<S>2022</S> Zion<S>6726</S> shall be deliverance<S>6413</S>, and there shall be holiness<S>6944</S>; and the house<S>1004</S> of Jacob<S>3290</S> shall possess<S>3423</S> their possessions<S>4180</S>. <sup>deliverance: or, they that escape</sup> <sup>there: or, it shall be holy</sup>
31_1_17.ESV: But in Mount Zion there shall be those who escape, and it shall be holy, and the house of Jacob shall possess their own possessions.
31_1_17.NASB: 'But on Mount Zion there will be those who escape, And it will be holy. And the house of Jacob will possess their possessions.
31_1_17.NLT:
“But Jerusalem will become a refuge for those who escape;
it will be a holy place.
And the people of Israel will come back
to reclaim their inheritance.
31_1_17.WEB: But in Mount Zion, there will be those who escape, and it will be holy. The house of Jacob will possess their possessions.
31_1_17.CSB17:
But there will be a deliverance on Mount Zion, ⓜ
and it will be holy; ⓝ
the house of Jacob will dispossess ⓐ
those who dispossessed them. [11] ⓑ
31_1_17.AMP:
"But on Mount Zion [in Jerusalem] there shall be [deliverance for] those who escape,
And it shall be holy [no pagan will defile it];
And the house of Jacob shall possess their [former] possessions.
31_1_17.MSG:
But not so on Mount Zion—there's respite there!
a safe and holy place!
The family of Jacob will take back their possessions
from those who took them from them.
31_1_17.NIV:
But on Mount Zion will be deliverance;
it will be holy,
and the house of Jacob
will possess its inheritance.
31_1_17.NRSVCE:
But on Mount Zion there shall be those that escape,
and it shall be holy;
and the house of Jacob shall take possession of those who dispossessed them.

31_1_18.NKJV: The house of Jacob shall be a fire, And the house of Joseph a flame; But the house of Esau shall be stubble; They shall kindle them and devour them, And no survivor shall remain of the house of Esau,” For the LORD has spoken.
31_1_18.KJV: And the house<S>1004</S> of Jacob<S>3290</S> shall be a fire<S>784</S>, and the house<S>1004</S> of Joseph<S>3130</S> a flame<S>3852</S>, and the house<S>1004</S> of Esau<S>6215</S> for stubble<S>7179</S>, and they shall kindle<S>1814</S> in them, and devour<S>398</S> them; and there shall not be any remaining<S>8300</S> of the house<S>1004</S> of Esau<S>6215</S>; for the LORD<S>3068</S> hath spoken<S>1696</S> it.
31_1_18.ESV: The house of Jacob shall be a fire, and the house of Joseph a flame, and the house of Esau stubble; they shall burn them and consume them, and there shall be no survivor for the house of Esau, for the LORD has spoken.
31_1_18.NASB: 'Then the house of Jacob will be a fire And the house of Joseph a flame; But the house of Esau [will be] as stubble. And they will set them on fire and consume them, So that there will be no survivor of the house of Esau,' For the LORD has spoken.
31_1_18.NLT:
The people of Israel will be a raging fire,
and Edom a field of dry stubble.
The descendants of Joseph will be a flame
roaring across the field, devouring everything.
There will be no survivors in Edom.
I, the LORD, have spoken!
31_1_18.WEB: The house of Jacob will be a fire, the house of Joseph a flame, and the house of Esau for stubble. They will burn among them, and devour them. There will not be any remaining to the house of Esau.” Indeed, Yahweh has spoken.
31_1_18.CSB17:
Then the house of Jacob will be a blazing fire, ⓒ
and the house of Joseph, a burning flame,
but the house of Esau will be stubble;
Jacob [12] will set them on fire and consume Edom. [13]
Therefore no survivor will remain ⓓ
of the house of Esau,
for the LORD has spoken.
31_1_18.AMP:
"Then the house of Jacob shall be a fire
And the house of Joseph a flame [in executing God's wrath];
But the house of Esau will be like stubble.
They (Jacob) shall set them on fire and consume them (the Edomites),
So that there shall be no survivor of the house of Esau,"
For the LORD has spoken.
31_1_18.MSG:
That's when the family of Jacob will catch fire,
the family of Joseph become fierce flame,
while the family of Esau will be straw.
Esau will go up in flames,
nothing left of Esau but a pile of ashes."
God said it, and it is so.
31_1_18.NIV:
The house of Jacob will be a fire
and the house of Joseph a flame;
the house of Esau will be stubble,
and they will set it on fire and consume it.
There will be no survivors
from the house of Esau.”
The Lord has spoken.
31_1_18.NRSVCE:
The house of Jacob shall be a fire,
the house of Joseph a flame,
and the house of Esau stubble;
they shall burn them and consume them,
and there shall be no survivor of the house of Esau;
— for the LORD has spoken.

31_1_19.NKJV: The South shall possess the mountains of Esau, And the Lowland shall possess Philistia. They shall possess the fields of Ephraim And the fields of Samaria. Benjamin shall possess Gilead.
31_1_19.KJV: And they of the south<S>5045</S> shall possess<S>3423</S> the mount<S>2022</S> of Esau<S>6215</S>; and they of the plain<S>8219</S> the Philistines<S>6430</S>: and they shall possess<S>3423</S> the fields<S>7704</S> of Ephraim<S>669</S>, and the fields<S>7704</S> of Samaria<S>8111</S>: and Benjamin<S>1144</S> shall possess Gilead<S>1568</S>.
31_1_19.ESV: Those of the Negeb shall possess Mount Esau, and those of the Shephelah shall possess the land of the Philistines; they shall possess the land of Ephraim and the land of Samaria, and Benjamin shall possess Gilead.
31_1_19.NASB: Then [those of] the Negev will possess the mountain of Esau, And [those of] the Shephelah the Philistine [plain]; Also, possess the territory of Ephraim and the territory of Samaria, And Benjamin [will possess] Gilead.
31_1_19.NLT:
“Then my people living in the Negev
will occupy the mountains of Edom.
Those living in the foothills of Judah
will possess the Philistine plains
and take over the fields of Ephraim and Samaria.
And the people of Benjamin
will occupy the land of Gilead.
31_1_19.WEB: Those of the South will possess the mountain of Esau, and those of the lowland, the Philistines. They will possess the field of Ephraim, and the field of Samaria. Benjamin will possess Gilead.
31_1_19.CSB17:
People from the Negev will possess ⓔ
the hill country of Esau; ⓕ
those from the Judean foothills will possess
the land of the Philistines. ⓖ
They [14] will possess
the territories of Ephraim and Samaria, ⓗ
while Benjamin will possess Gilead.
31_1_19.AMP:
Then those of the Negev shall possess the mountain of Esau,
And those of the Shephelah [shall possess] the Philistine plain;
Also, [they shall] possess the fields of Ephraim and the fields of Samaria,
And Benjamin will possess Gilead [across the Jordan River].
31_1_19.MSG:
People from the south will take over the Esau mountains;
people from the foothills will overrun the Philistines.
They'll take the farms of Ephraim and Samaria,
and Benjamin will take Gilead.
31_1_19.NIV:
People from the Negev will occupy
the mountains of Esau,
and people from the foothills will possess
the land of the Philistines.
They will occupy the fields of Ephraim and Samaria,
and Benjamin will possess Gilead.
31_1_19.NRSVCE:
Those of the Negeb shall possess Mount Esau,
and those of the Shephelah the land of the Philistines;
they shall possess the land of Ephraim and the land of Samaria,
and Benjamin shall possess Gilead.

31_1_20.NKJV: And the captives of this host of the children of Israel Shall possess the land of the Canaanites As far as Zarephath. The captives of Jerusalem who are in Sepharad Shall possess the cities of the South.
31_1_20.KJV: And the captivity<S>1546</S> of this host<S>2426</S> of the children<S>1121</S> of Israel<S>3478</S> shall possess that of the Canaanites<S>3669</S>, even unto Zarephath<S>6886</S>; and the captivity<S>1546</S> of Jerusalem<S>3389</S>, which is in Sepharad<S>5614</S>, shall possess<S>3423</S> the cities<S>5892</S> of the south<S>5045</S>. <sup>which: or, shall possess that which is in</sup>
31_1_20.ESV: The exiles of this host of the people of Israel shall possess the land of the Canaanites as far as Zarephath, and the exiles of Jerusalem who are in Sepharad shall possess the cities of the Negeb.
31_1_20.NASB: And the exiles of this host of the sons of Israel, Who are [among] the Canaanites as far as Zarephath, And the exiles of Jerusalem who are in Sepharad Will possess the cities of the Negev.
31_1_20.NLT:
The exiles of Israel will return to their land
and occupy the Phoenician coast as far north as Zarephath.
The captives from Jerusalem exiled in the north
will return home and resettle the towns of the Negev.
31_1_20.WEB: The captives of this army of the children of Israel, who are among the Canaanites, will possess even to Zarephath; and the captives of Jerusalem, who are in Sepharad, will possess the cities of the Negev.
31_1_20.CSB17:
The exiles of the Israelites who are in Halah [15]
and who are among the Canaanites as far as Zarephath ⓘ
as well as the exiles of Jerusalem who are in Sepharad
will possess the cities of the Negev. ⓙ
31_1_20.AMP:
And the exiles of this host of the sons (descendants) of Israel
Who are among the Canaanites as far as Zarephath,
And the exiles of Jerusalem who are in Sepharad
Shall possess the cities of the Negev.
31_1_20.MSG:
Earlier, Israelite exiles will come back
and take Canaanite land to the north at Zarephath.
Jerusalem exiles from the far northwest in Sepharad
will come back and take the cities in the south.
31_1_20.NIV:
This company of Israelite exiles who are in Canaan
will possess the land as far as Zarephath;
the exiles from Jerusalem who are in Sepharad
will possess the towns of the Negev.
31_1_20.NRSVCE:
The exiles of the Israelites who are in Halah
shall possess Phoenicia as far as Zarephath;
and the exiles of Jerusalem who are in Sepharad
shall possess the towns of the Negeb.

31_1_21.NKJV: Then saviors shall come to Mount Zion To judge the mountains of Esau, And the kingdom shall be the LORD’s.
31_1_21.KJV: And saviours<S>3467</S> shall come up<S>5927</S> on mount<S>2022</S> Zion<S>6726</S> to judge<S>8199</S> the mount<S>2022</S> of Esau<S>6215</S>; and the kingdom<S>4410</S> shall be the LORD'S<S>3068</S>.
31_1_21.ESV: Saviors shall go up to Mount Zion to rule Mount Esau, and the kingdom shall be the LORD's.
31_1_21.NASB: The deliverers will ascend Mount Zion To judge the mountain of Esau, And the kingdom will be the LORD'S.
31_1_21.NLT:
Those who have been rescued will go up to Mount Zion in Jerusalem
to rule over the mountains of Edom.
And the LORD himself will be king!”
31_1_21.WEB: Saviors will go up on Mount Zion to judge the mountains of Esau, and the kingdom will be Yahweh’s.
31_1_21.CSB17:
Saviors [16] will ascend Mount Zion ⓚ
to rule over the hill country of Esau,
but the kingdom will be the LORD’s. ⓛ
31_1_21.AMP:
The deliverers shall go up on Mount Zion
To rule and judge the mountain of Esau,
And the kingdom and the kingship shall be the Lord's.
31_1_21.MSG:
The remnant of the saved in Mount Zion
will go into the mountains of Esau
And rule justly and fairly,
a rule that honors God's kingdom.
31_1_21.NIV:
Deliverers will go up on Mount Zion
to govern the mountains of Esau.
And the kingdom will be the Lord’s.
31_1_21.NRSVCE:
Those who have been saved shall go up to Mount Zion
to rule Mount Esau;
and the kingdom shall be the LORD’s.
